artifacts.claimText: For student assessment, TU Dublin guidance says generative AI can only be used in ways approved in advance by the lecturer, and that failure to follow the guidelines or inappropriate AI use may result in disciplinary action as a breach of academic integrity.
artifacts.normalizedValue: Student GenAI use requires advance lecturer approval for assessments

artifacts.sourceTitle: Guidelines on the responsible use of Generative AI in Teaching, Learning, Feedback and Assessment in TU Dublin
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: To support academic integrity, GenAI can only be used by students in ways that are approved in advance by their lecturer. Failure to adhere to these guidelines or the inappropriate use of AI, as determined by the relevant Faculty, may result in disciplinary action as it is a breach of academic integrity.

artifacts.claimText: TU Dublin's research GenAI guidance says use of generative AI tools must follow applicable institutional, national, EU and international laws, regulations, and data protection standards, and that users are wholly responsible for checking the veracity, accuracy, creative merit, falsification, fabrication, and plagiarism risks of model output.
artifacts.normalizedValue: Research GenAI use must meet law/data protection obligations and user responsibility

artifacts.sourceTitle: Responsible use of Generative AI in Research V1.0
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Use of generative AI systems/tools must follow applicable institutional, national, EU and international laws, regulations and data protection standards. The user of generative AI systems/tools is wholly responsible for ensuring the veracity, accuracy and/or creative merit of the output generated by the model, and for assessing the potential for falsification, fabrication and plagiarism due to the use of the system.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Principles for use of Generative AI, Principles 1 and 3

artifacts.sourceTitle: Responsible use of Generative AI in Research V1.0
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: While a broad range of systems and tools are available, only the following should be used in the context of research activities where the information is sensitive, confidential or where there are/may be intellectual property issues, including during peer review practices: Systems which by default do not retain data or which permit user to disable data retention. Systems where the data is created and saved on closed/bespoke University hardware platforms.

artifacts.title: Technological University Dublin staged AI policy evidence
artifacts.summary: Staged official-source evidence for Technological University Dublin identifies TU Dublin guidance on GenAI in teaching, assessment communication, student academic integrity, personal data handling, and research use including authorship and sensitive research contexts. Claims are agent-reviewed candidates only and are not canonical public claims.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
This staged run uses official TU Dublin public sources only and does not publish canonical claims without later review.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
